ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tests" (
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test case */,
  "test_name" TEXT /* Name of the test case */,
  "test_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test type */,
  "test_status_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test status */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Error message if the test failed */,
  "owner_id" INTEGER /* ID of the owner of the test case */,
  "duration" REAL /* Duration of the test in minutes */,
  "test_suite_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test suite */,
  "test_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the test case */,
  "test_environment" TEXT /* Environment in which the test was run */,
  "test_created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the test case was created */,
  "test_updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the test case was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tests_test_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_type_id") REFERENCES test_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tests_test_suite_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_suite_id") REFERENCES test_suites ("suite_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tests_owner_id FOREIGN KEY ("owner_id") REFERENCES owners ("owner_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tests_test_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_status_id") REFERENCES test_status ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the test type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the test type */,
  "test_type_group" TEXT /* Group to which the test type belongs */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the test status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the test status */,
  "status_color" TEXT /* Color code for the test status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "owners" (
  "owner_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each owner */,
  "owner_name" TEXT /* Name of the owner */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the owner */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the owner */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the owner was added to the system */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the owner's information was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("owner_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test result */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case */,
  "test_run_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test run */,
  "result" TEXT /* Result of the test (e.g., passed, failed) */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Error message if the test failed */,
  "test_run_date" TEXT /* Date of the test run */,
  "test_environment" TEXT /* Environment in which the test was run */,
  "test_duration" REAL /* Duration of the test run in minutes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES tests ("test_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_cases" (
  "case_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test case */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case */,
  "case_name" TEXT /* Name of the test case */,
  "case_description" TEXT /* Description of the test case */,
  "test_case_steps" TEXT /* Detailed list of steps for the test case */,
  "test_case_preconditions" TEXT /* Preconditions for the test case */,
  "test_case_expected_results" TEXT /* Expected results of the test case */,
  "test_case_status" TEXT /* Status of the test case (e.g., active, deprecated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_cases_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES tests ("test_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_suites" (
  "suite_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test suite */,
  "suite_name" TEXT /* Name of the test suite */,
  "suite_description" TEXT /* Description of the test suite */,
  "suite_owner_id" INTEGER /* ID of the owner of the test suite */,
  "suite_created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the test suite was created */,
  "suite_updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the test suite was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("suite_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_suites_suite_owner_id FOREIGN KEY ("suite_owner_id") REFERENCES owners ("owner_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_suite_tests" (
  "suite_test_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each suite-test pair */,
  "suite_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test suite */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("suite_test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_suite_tests_suite_id FOREIGN KEY ("suite_id") REFERENCES test_suites ("suite_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_suite_tests_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES tests ("test_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, QA engineer, project manager) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user was added to the system */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user's information was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the test data */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the test data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "access_user_agent" TEXT /* User agent (e.g., browser) used for access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the test case */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date when the version was created */,
  "version_description" TEXT /* Description of the changes made in the version */,
  "version_creator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES tests ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_version_creator_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_creator_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "version_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each change event */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the version */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was made */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Description of the change */,
  "change_creator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "change_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the change was made */,
  "change_user_agent" TEXT /* User agent (e.g., browser) used for the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_version_history_change_creator_id FOREIGN KEY ("change_creator_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_version_history_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES tests ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_version_history_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "role_hierarchy" INTEGER /* Hierarchy level of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "permission_description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  "permission_hierarchy" INTEGER /* Hierarchy level of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user-role pair */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_permissions" (
  "role_permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role-permission pair */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* ID of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_role_permissions_permission_id FOREIGN KEY ("permission_id") REFERENCES permissions ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_role_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)
